title: A Gunman Killed a Montreal Officer at the Hilton
script: A Montreal cop was killed! Monday morning, June 22. Someone in Montreal's Côte-des-Neiges neighborhood called 911: there's a man in the Hilton with a gun pointed out the window. Officers responded. Constable Mohamed Lamine Benredouane — 34 years old, on the force since 2021 — was shot and killed. A second officer was wounded and is in stable condition. A civilian also died. The gunman was shot dead at the scene. It was the first time a Montreal police officer had been killed in the line of duty in 24 years. Investigators ruled out terrorism. They haven't confirmed what triggered the shooting. Constable Benredouane had spent 5 years protecting that city. What brought the gunman to that Hilton window?
